novel_features.name: Creative-Fatigue Observatory
novel_features.description: Detect ads whose CPM is drifting up, frequency is climbing, and CTR is falling across a configurable window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires daily insights time series joined locally. A single API call returns the current value, never the slope. Local SQLite makes the slope queryable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to decide which ads to retire. Spending on a fatigued creative is the highest-leverage waste in any account.

novel_features.name: Creative Decay Curve
novel_features.description: Compare a creative's first-impression CTR against its current CTR, with projected dead-date.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both endpoints of the historical CTR curve, which only exists in the local store. The live API returns only current value.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for retire/refresh decisions on a single creative. The slope and projected dead-date are the deciding factor.

novel_features.name: Audience Overlap Cartographer
novel_features.description: Pairwise overlap percentages across custom audiences, with cannibalization recommendations.
novel_features.rationale: Pure API returns pair overlap per call; cross-pair scoring and recommendation requires composing multiple results, only feasible offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when ad strategy is mature and ROAS feels diluted across similar audiences. Surfaces consolidate-or-exclude decisions.

novel_features.name: Learning-Phase Forensics
novel_features.description: Surface adsets stuck in algorithmic learning >N days with root-cause hint (budget too low, audience too narrow, events too sparse).
novel_features.rationale: API returns learning_stage_info per adset but not the filter or the cross-field root-cause join. Local store enables both.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when ROAS collapses across multiple adsets simultaneously. Stuck-in-learning adsets eat budget without optimizing.

novel_features.name: Spend Reconciliation
novel_features.description: Per-day diff between Meta-reported account spend and sum-of-insights spend; flags days where attribution drift exceeds threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Reconciliation requires daily series on both sides and a per-day join. The API does not expose 'reported spend vs sum of insights' as a single call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use monthly for attribution audits. Surfaces specific days where Meta and insights disagree, usually delayed conversion attribution.

novel_features.name: Bottleneck Surfacer
novel_features.description: Highest-spend adsets with worst ROAS, ranked with 'why' column joining learning state and effective status.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-adset comparison plus multi-field 'why' join cannot be done in one API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use weekly to decide what to pause. Highest-leverage targets surface first.

novel_features.name: Stale Active Ads
novel_features.description: Active ads with zero impressions in N days.
novel_features.rationale: Requires scanning historical impressions, which is daily-series data the live API doesn't surface in a single call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use quarterly for account hygiene. Active ads with zero impressions are usually misconfigured or post-deletion zombies.

novel_features.name: Account Inventory Roll-up
novel_features.description: Group every ad in an account by effective_status, surface ads where configured status='ACTIVE' but effective_status='WITH_ISSUES' or 'DISAPPROVED'.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the configured-vs-effective join across all ads in an account, which the Meta UI buries behind per-ad clicks.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use first thing every morning. DISAPPROVED-but-still-ACTIVE ads cost real spend without delivering.

narrative.headline: The first agent-native Meta Ads CLI with local SQLite history and compound queries the live API cannot answer.
narrative.value_prop: Read-only insights CLI for Meta Marketing API focused on creative-fatigue detection, audience overlap analysis, and attribution forensics. Daily insights land in a local SQLite store so commands like `fatigue`, `decay`, `learning`, and `reconcile` can join across history. Single META_ACCESS_TOKEN env var with `ads_read` scope.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authenticate with a Meta access token in the META_ACCESS_TOKEN env var. Generate from Graph API Explorer (User Access Token, 1-2h lifespan) for ad-hoc use, or Business Manager System Users (no expiry) for repeat use. Grant ads_read scope only — never ads_management or any write permission. Token discovery via /me/adaccounts lists every account accessible to the token; no account ID configuration required.

quickstart.comment: Verify the token and config are wired correctly before any API calls.
List every accessible ad account (returns paged data array with id, name, currency, etc.).
List campaigns for the test account. Run live; --data-source auto caches results in the local SQLite store for fatigue/decay/learning/reconcile.
Surface ads with creative-fatigue signals across the last 14 days.
Group every ad by effective_status, flagging configured-vs-effective mismatches.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty ad_accounts list with a valid token
troubleshoots.fix: Business Manager assignment missing. Open Business Settings → Ad Accounts → Add People and assign your user (or System User) with View permission.
Token expired. Regenerate a User Access Token in Graph API Explorer, or generate a System User token in Business Manager for long-lived use.
The Meta App backing this token is deactivated. Reactivate at developers.facebook.com/apps/, or generate a token from a different active app.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty insights despite valid token and active ads
troubleshoots.fix: Insights require time_range or date_preset; there is no default window. Pass --date-preset last_7d or --time-range {"since":"2026-01-01","until":"2026-01-31"}.
Some breakdown combinations are mutually exclusive. age+gender works; age+placement does not. Use one breakdown group at a time, or split into multiple calls.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need creative-fatigue diagnostics, audience overlap cartography, learning-phase forensics, or attribution drift detection across Meta ad accounts. Best when paired with a daily sync into the local store so trend queries are instant. Read-only by design — never used to modify campaigns, budgets, or audiences.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to create, pause, modify, or delete campaigns, adsets, ads, audiences, or creatives — it is read-only.
Do not use this CLI for budget management or bid-strategy changes — that is what Meta Ads Manager or facebook-business-sdk are for.
Do not use this CLI for write-side audience uploads — custom audience creation from customer files is out of scope.

recipes.title: Triage creative fatigue across a campaign
recipes.explanation: Returns one row per ad with CPM slope, current frequency, CTR slope, and a retire/keep verdict. Narrow output by selecting only the fatigue-relevant fields to keep agent context bounded.

recipes.title: Find audience cannibalization risk
recipes.explanation: Returns pairwise overlap percentages and a recommendation per pair. Anything above 30% is a candidate for consolidation or exclusion.

recipes.title: Spot stuck-in-learning adsets
recipes.explanation: Surfaces adsets where learning_stage_info has been LEARNING for >7 days. The 'why' column hints at budget/audience/event sparsity.

recipes.title: Daily inventory roll-up first thing
recipes.explanation: Groups every ad by effective_status. The DISAPPROVED-but-configured-ACTIVE class is the highest-leverage daily finding.

recipes.title: Monthly spend reconciliation
recipes.explanation: Per-day diff between Meta-reported account spend and sum-of-insights spend over the last 30 days. Flags days where drift exceeds 5%.

trigger_phrases: which Meta ads should I retire
